test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Access the site as a guest via 'Access as a guest' button
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: Unauthenticated browsing interface is displayed and a 'Guest' indicator is visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Cookies notice to view cookie usage information
steps: 1. Click the 'Cookies notice' button
test_cases.expected_result: A dialog is displayed showing cookie usage information

test_cases.test_case: Verify 'Lost password?' link is present but disabled on test site
steps: 1. Check that the 'Lost password?' link is visible and disabled
test_cases.expected_result: 'Lost password?' link is visible and disabled on the page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Username blank (required text field representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; Password field is cleared (becomes empty); user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Password blank (password field required representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; Username field retains the entered value; user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Username and Password fields each display inline validation errors indicating they are required; no redirect occurs; Login form remains displayed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt login with invalid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not redirect to Dashboard; an inline error is shown on the form indicating the credentials are invalid; Password field is cleared; Username field retains the entered value; user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Click disabled 'Lost password?' link (precondition: link disabled on test site)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Test site configuration: 'Lost password?' link disabled
steps: 1. Click the "Lost password?" link
test_cases.expected_result: The "Lost password?" link is visibly disabled/non-interactive (e.g., greyed out) and clicking it does not navigate or open a password recovery page; no change in page state occurs

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username and Password (200+ chars) submission
preconditions: User is on the Login page
There are no known valid credentials for the input values to be used (submission will be treated as invalid)
steps: 1. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Username field
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission is attempted and is blocked / error shown: an inline error message is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered very long string (visible in the Username input)

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password containing only whitespace characters
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter one or more whitespace characters (e.g., space characters) in the Username field
2. Enter one or more whitespace characters in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown: inline error message appears indicating credentials must be provided; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the exact whitespace characters entered (visible in the input) — demonstrating how the UI treats whitespace-only input

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password with special characters and Unicode (emoji)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
There are no known valid credentials for the input values to be used (submission will be treated as invalid)
steps: 1. Enter a string containing special characters and Unicode (e.g., emoji, accented letters, symbols) in the Username field
2. Enter a string containing special characters and Unicode in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown: inline error message is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the special-character/Unicode string exactly as entered (visible in the input)

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest then navigate back to Login page
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
2. Observe that unauthenticated browsing access is granted (page content for guest browsing is shown)
3. Use the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Guest access succeeds: user is granted unauthenticated browsing access after clicking the button; after pressing Back, the Login page is shown and the Login form fields are empty (Username and Password not pre-filled with prior guest session values)

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode shows Reset and Add Block controls
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the Edit Mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is visible at the top right and the '+ Add a block' button is visible below the Dashboard heading

test_cases.test_case: Click '+ Add a block' in Edit mode opens Block Library page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the Edit Mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
3. Click the '+ Add a block' button
test_cases.expected_result: Block Library page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Timeline block shows move handle and options menu entries in Edit mode
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the Edit Mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
3. Locate the Timeline block and verify the Move handle is visible
4. Click the Timeline block's options (three-dot) menu
test_cases.expected_result: The options menu shows entries 'Configure', 'Move', and 'Delete'

test_cases.test_case: Search in Timeline filters activities by name or type
preconditions: Dashboard contains activities in the Timeline block
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Enter <search term> in the Timeline block's Search activities field
3. Press Enter or trigger the search
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only activities matching <search term>; unrelated activities are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows empty state when no items are in the selected time range
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Select <time range with no items> from the Timeline block's Time Range dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline block displays its empty state indicating there are no upcoming activities in the selected range

test_cases.test_case: Click 'New event' in Calendar block opens New Event modal
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'New event' button
test_cases.expected_result: New Event modal is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Click Next month updates the calendar heading to the next month
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Next month' button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to display the next month and year

test_cases.test_case: Click Previous month updates the calendar heading to the previous month
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Previous month' button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to display the previous month and year

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Full calendar' opens the Full Calendar view page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Full calendar' link
test_cases.expected_result: Full Calendar view page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Import or export calendars' opens Calendar Data Management page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Import or export calendars' link
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar Data Management page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Reset page to default' restores the Dashboard to its default layout
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the Edit Mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
3. Click the 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard layout returns to the default arrangement with Timeline and Calendar blocks in their original positions

test_cases.test_case: Edit-mode controls hidden when Edit Mode is off
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
Edit_Mode == false (the Edit mode checkbox is unchecked)
steps: 1. Ensure the Edit_Mode checkbox is unchecked
2. Look for the 'Reset page to default' button at the top right
3. Look for the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
4. Inspect a block (e.g., Timeline block) for a move handle ('Move') and the three-dot options menu
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is not visible; the '+ Add a block' button is not visible; the block move handle ('Move') is not visible; the three-dot options menu is not visible. No edit controls are present and the Dashboard remains unchanged (no edit UI is shown).

test_cases.test_case: Cannot perform block delete when Edit Mode is off
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
A Timeline block exists on the Dashboard
Edit_Mode == false (the Edit mode checkbox is unchecked)
steps: 1. Ensure the Edit_Mode checkbox is unchecked
2. Attempt to open the Timeline block options menu (three-dot menu)
3. If the options menu is not visible, attempt to click a Delete action for the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block options menu is not visible / cannot be opened when Edit Mode is off; the Delete action cannot be invoked. The Timeline block remains present (not deleted) and no deletion confirmation is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Timeline empty state shown when there are zero items in the selected time range
preconditions: Timeline block is visible on the Dashboard
There are NO activities within the selected time range (test data/state has 0 items_in_range)
steps: 1. Click the Time_Range dropdown in the Timeline block
2. Select the "Next 7 days" option
3. Wait for the Timeline block to refresh
4. Observe the Timeline block content area
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays the Empty_State element (empty state panel is visible) and the timeline item list shows zero entries — this succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows a single activity when exactly one item exists in the selected time range (empty state hidden)
preconditions: Timeline block is visible on the Dashboard
Exactly ONE activity exists within the selected time range (test data/state has items_in_range == 1)
steps: 1. Click the Time_Range dropdown in the Timeline block
2. Select the "Next 7 days" option
3. Wait for the Timeline block to refresh
4. Observe the Timeline block content area
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays exactly one activity entry in the timeline item list and the Empty_State element is not visible — this succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Search_Activities accepts a very long search string (200+ characters) and handles it without UI failure
preconditions: Timeline block is visible on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activities field in the Timeline block
2. Type a string of 200+ characters into Search_Activities
3. Press Enter (or trigger the search action)
4. Observe the Search_Activities field value and the Timeline block content area
test_cases.expected_result: The Search_Activities field displays the entered long string and the search executes without UI error or crash; the UI either shows matching results or displays the Empty_State if there are no matches — the search action succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid toggle of Edit_Mode: options menu interaction blocked when Edit_Mode is turned off before menu click
preconditions: Timeline block (or another block with edit_controls) is present
steps: 1. Click the Edit_Mode checkbox to toggle it ON
2. Immediately (within 1 second) click the Edit_Mode checkbox again to toggle it OFF
3. Attempt to click the Timeline_Block.options_menu (three-dot menu) on the Timeline block
4. Observe whether the options menu opens
test_cases.expected_result: Because Edit_Mode was toggled OFF before interacting, the options_menu is not visible and the attempt to open it is blocked; no menu appears and no UI error is shown — the action is blocked.

test_cases.test_case: Open course from course card link
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Course Name link on the card for <target course>
test_cases.expected_result: 'navigates to course main page' — The browser navigates to the course main page for <target course>; the course main page is displayed (redirect to the course page is observed).

test_cases.test_case: Star a course using the three-dot menu pins it to the top
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid and is not starred
steps: 1. Click the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
2. Click 'Star this course' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: 'pins course to the top' — The card for <target course> appears as the first card in the Courses grid (pinned to the top) and is visually identifiable as starred.

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view using the three-dot menu
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
2. Click 'Remove from view' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: 'hides course from view without unenrolling' — The card for <target course> is no longer visible in the Courses grid; other course cards remain visible.

test_cases.test_case: Filter courses by Status = Starred
preconditions: At least one course is starred and present in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Status dropdown in the Controls Bar
2. Select 'Starred' from the Status dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only starred courses; non-starred course cards are not visible and at least one starred course card is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Search for courses using the Search field
preconditions: Multiple courses with distinguishable names/categories are present in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Enter <search term> in the Search field in the Controls Bar
2. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search control)
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only course cards whose Course Name or category match <search term>; unrelated course cards are no longer visible.

test_cases.test_case: Change layout to List view
preconditions: Multiple courses are present in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Layout dropdown in the Controls Bar
2. Select 'List' from the Layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid switches to List layout: courses are displayed in a vertical list where each row shows the course name and category.

test_cases.test_case: Apply a Sort option and observe reordered courses
preconditions: Multiple courses with different <sort key> values are present in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Sort dropdown in the Controls Bar
2. Select <sort option> from the Sort dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid updates to reflect the selected <sort option>; course cards are displayed in the chosen sort order.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access My Courses page
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the My Courses page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page and the Login form is displayed; the My Courses grid and course cards are not visible / accessible

test_cases.test_case: Click Course Name when course main page cannot be loaded (navigation failure)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student and is enrolled in at least one course
The course main page endpoint is unavailable or returns an error
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page while signed in
2. Click the Course Name link on a course card
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user remains on the My Courses page; an inline error banner or toast is shown indicating the course page could not be loaded; the course main page is not opened

test_cases.test_case: Search with a very long string (200+ chars)
preconditions: User is on the My Courses page
steps: 1. Focus the Search field in the Controls_Bar
2. Enter <a very long search string (200+ characters)> in the Search field
3. Press Enter (or click the Search submit control)
test_cases.expected_result: Entering the very long string succeeds; the search operation completes and Courses_Grid either displays matching course cards or shows a visible 'No courses found' message. The UI does not crash or visually break.

test_cases.test_case: Search with special characters, emoji, and leading/trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is on the My Courses page and at least one course exists
steps: 1. Focus the Search field in the Controls_Bar
2. Enter <a search string containing special characters and emoji, with leading and trailing spaces> in the Search field
3. Press Enter (or click the Search submit control)
test_cases.expected_result: Search with special characters and whitespace succeeds; the search is executed and Courses_Grid either shows matching results or a visible 'No courses found' message. Leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed in the effective search (visible by results) or clearly ignored without an error; no error message is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view then confirm visibility via Status filter 'Hidden'
preconditions: User is on the My Courses page and there is at least one visible course card
steps: 1. Locate a specific course card in Courses_Grid
2. Click the Three_Dot_Menu on that course card
3. Click the 'Remove from view' row action
4. Observe the Courses_Grid immediately after the action
5. Open the Status_Filter dropdown in the Controls_Bar
6. Select 'Hidden' from the Status_Filter options
7. Observe the Courses_Grid after selecting 'Hidden'
test_cases.expected_result: 'Remove from view' succeeds; the course card is removed from the current (default) Courses_Grid view immediately. Selecting Status_Filter='Hidden' succeeds; the previously removed course card appears in Courses_Grid when 'Hidden' is active, showing that the course is hidden from default view but still present in the Hidden filter.

test_cases.test_case: Star a course, navigate into it, then use browser back — verify pin persists and no duplicate entries
preconditions: User is on the My Courses page and there is at least one course card that is not currently starred
steps: 1. Locate a non-starred course card in Courses_Grid
2. Click the Three_Dot_Menu on that course card
3. Click the 'Star this course' row action
4. Observe the Courses_Grid to confirm the course is pinned to the top position
5. Click the Course_Name link on that same course card to navigate to the course main page
6. Use the browser Back button
7. Observe the My Courses page and Courses_Grid after returning
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Star this course' succeeds; the course is pinned to the top of Courses_Grid immediately. Navigating to the course main page and pressing the browser Back button succeeds in returning to My Courses; the pinned course remains at the top and is shown only once (no duplicate entries are created).

test_cases.test_case: Course header and navigation tab bar are visible
preconditions: Course has at least one section with at least one activity/resource
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
test_cases.expected_result: The course name is displayed as the page heading and the navigation tab bar is visible below the heading

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a single section to collapse its items
preconditions: Course has at least one expanded section with visible items
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Click the Toggle section collapse control for a section
test_cases.expected_result: That section's item list is hidden and the section's chevron/indicator shows the collapsed state

test_cases.test_case: Collapse all sections using the Collapse all link
preconditions: Course has multiple expanded sections with visible items
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Click the 'Collapse all' link in the top-right of the sections area
test_cases.expected_result: All sections collapsed; every section's item list is hidden and each section's chevron/indicator shows the collapsed state

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity or resource from a section
preconditions: Course has at least one section containing at least one activity/resource
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Click the Activity or Resource link for an item inside a section
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to the activity/resource page and the activity/resource page header is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit Mode as a teacher
preconditions: Course has at least one section
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Click the 'Enable Edit Mode' button
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode enabled; edit controls are visible on sections and items indicating edit mode is active

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit Mode button is not visible to students
preconditions: Course has at least one section
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Enable Edit Mode' button is not visible on the Course page

test_cases.test_case: Student user cannot enable Edit Mode (role precondition)
preconditions: User is authenticated with Student role
Course Page contains at least one section
steps: 1. Log in as a user with the Student role
2. Navigate to the Course Page
3. Look for the 'Enable Edit Mode' control
4. If the control is present, attempt to click 'Enable Edit Mode'
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Enable Edit Mode' control is not available to the Student role: the control is not visible on the Course Page (or is rendered disabled). Clicking it does not enable edit mode; no edit UI or editing controls appear and the page remains in view-only mode. (The attempted action is blocked by role precondition.)

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot enable Edit Mode (requires authenticated teacher role)
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out
2. Navigate to the Course Page
3. Attempt to access or activate 'Enable Edit Mode' (click if visible or attempt the control's URL)
test_cases.expected_result: Access to 'Enable Edit Mode' is blocked for unauthenticated users: the user is redirected to the Login page or shown a login prompt when attempting the action; edit mode is not enabled and no edit UI is shown on the Course Page.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid repeated clicks on 'Collapse all' are idempotent
preconditions: Course page is open with >1 sections visible
User is on the course page (any role)
steps: 1. Click the 'Collapse all' link
2. Immediately click the 'Collapse all' link a second time (before the collapse animation completes)
test_cases.expected_result: All sections end in the collapsed state; the second 'Collapse all' click does not cause an error and the action succeeds idempotently — UI shows all section chevrons in collapsed state and no error message is displayed

test_cases.test_case: 'Collapse all' with zero sections present
preconditions: Course page is open for a course that has zero sections (empty sections repeating group)
User is on the course page (any role)
steps: 1. Observe that no section rows are listed
2. Click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: 'Collapse all' action succeeds with no side effects; page remains showing no sections and no error is shown — a visible no-sections state (empty list) remains and no JavaScript error banner appears

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive toggles of a single section stabilize to the final user action
preconditions: Course page is open with at least one section present
User is on the course page (any role)
steps: 1. Click the section's chevron (Toggle section collapse) to start a collapse or expand
2. Immediately click the same section's chevron two more times in quick succession
3. Wait for animations to finish
test_cases.expected_result: After the final toggle action completes, the section is in the state corresponding to the last click and the action succeeds; the UI shows the section collapsed or expanded according to the final toggle and no error message or inconsistent partial-UI state is visible

test_cases.test_case: Click an item link while its parent section is collapsing (timing race)
preconditions: Course page is open with a section that contains at least one Section_Items entry
User is on the course page (any role)
steps: 1. Click the section's chevron to initiate collapse
2. Immediately (during the collapse animation) click the Activity or Resource Item_Link within that same section
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation from the Item_Link is blocked if the parent section has become non-interactive during collapse; no navigation occurs and the Item_Link does not open the activity/resource while collapse is in progress — the action is blocked / error shown (no new page opens and no silent navigation happens)

test_cases.test_case: Student attempts to enable Edit Mode (precondition role boundary)
preconditions: User is signed in with a student role
steps: 1. Click the 'Enable Edit Mode' button
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked / error shown: Edit mode is not enabled for the student and the page remains in read-only view; a visible denial (inline error, toast, or no change) is shown and no edit UI appears

test_cases.test_case: Teacher enables Edit Mode then immediately triggers 'Collapse all' (rapid state transition)
preconditions: User is signed in with a teacher role
Course page is open with multiple sections visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Enable Edit Mode' button
2. Immediately after the UI indicates edit mode is starting, click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: Both state transitions succeed: edit mode becomes enabled and 'Collapse all' collapses sections while edit UI is active — the page shows the edit-mode indicator and all sections in collapsed state; no action is blocked and no error message is shown

test_cases.test_case: Very long course name (heading) with special characters and emoji displayed on Course Page
preconditions: A course exists where the course name (heading) contains a very long string with special characters and emoji (length significantly greater than typical heading length)
User navigates to that course page (any role)
steps: 1. Open the course page for the course with the very long heading
test_cases.expected_result: Displaying a very long course name succeeds: the heading renders without breaking the layout (text wraps or is truncated with visible ellipsis per UI rules) and no overlap/overflow of other UI elements occurs — the page remains usable and no visual corruption or error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Add a filter condition row
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add condition' link in the Filter Builder
test_cases.expected_result: A new condition row appears in the Conditions area with an empty Attribute dropdown and an empty Value field

test_cases.test_case: Apply a single filter condition
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add condition' link in the Filter Builder
2. Select <attribute> from the new condition's Attribute dropdown
3. Enter <value> into the new condition's Value field
4. Click the 'Apply filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: applies filter conditions to participants list

test_cases.test_case: Clear all filters and reset toggles
preconditions: At least one filter condition is active and alphabetical filter/toggle may be set
steps: 1. Click the 'Clear filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: clears all filter conditions and resets Any toggle and alphabetical filters

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants by first-name initial using alphabet buttons
steps: 1. Click the 'A' button in the First name alphabetical filter
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only rows with first names starting with 'A'; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants by last-name initial using alphabet buttons
steps: 1. Click the 'M' button in the Last name alphabetical filter
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only rows with last names starting with 'M'; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Sort participants by First/Last name column
steps: 1. Click the 'First/Last name' column header in the Participants table to sort
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table is sorted alphabetically by First/Last name; rows reorder accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Student views a participant profile via name link
steps: 1. Click a participant's name link in the First/Last name column
test_cases.expected_result: Participant profile page is displayed for <selected participant>

test_cases.test_case: Instructor enrolls a participant using row action
steps: 1. Open the row actions menu for a participant in the Participants table
2. Click the 'Enrol' row action
test_cases.expected_result: Roles column for the participant updates to reflect enrolment

test_cases.test_case: Instructor unenrols a participant using row action
steps: 1. Open the row actions menu for a participant in the Participants table
2. Click the 'Unenrol' row action
test_cases.expected_result: Roles column for the participant updates to reflect unenrolment

test_cases.test_case: Instructor opens Edit Roles for a participant
steps: 1. Open the row actions menu for a participant in the Participants table
2. Click the 'Edit Roles' row action
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Roles dialog is displayed for the selected participant

test_cases.test_case: Instructor bulk-enrol selected participants
preconditions: At least two participants are visible in the table
steps: 1. Check the Select checkboxes for two participants in the Participants table
2. Click the 'Enrol Selected' bulk action
test_cases.expected_result: Roles column for the selected participants updates to reflect enrolment

test_cases.test_case: Instructor bulk-unenrol selected participants
preconditions: At least two participants are visible in the table
steps: 1. Check the Select checkboxes for two participants in the Participants table
2. Click the 'Unenrol Selected' bulk action
test_cases.expected_result: Roles column for the selected participants updates to reflect unenrolment

test_cases.test_case: Apply filters with required Attribute dropdown left blank (single condition)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add condition' link
2. Leave the Attribute dropdown blank for the new condition row (do not select a value)
3. Click the 'Apply filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Attribute dropdown field in the condition row displays an inline validation error indicating it is required (e.g. 'This field is required'). The Apply filters action is blocked; filters are not applied and the participants list remains unchanged; no navigation occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Apply filters with all required Attribute dropdowns blank (multiple condition rows)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add condition' link
2. Click the '+ Add condition' link again to add a second row
3. In condition row 1, leave the Attribute dropdown blank
4. In condition row 2, leave the Attribute dropdown blank
5. Click the 'Apply filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: Each Attribute dropdown in every condition row shows an inline validation error indicating it is required. The Apply filters action is blocked; no filters are applied and the participants list remains unchanged; no partial application occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Student role cannot access 'Edit Roles' row action
preconditions: User is on the Participants page
steps: 1. Open the row action menu for a participant in the Participants table
2. Attempt to click the 'Edit Roles' row action
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Edit Roles' row action is not available to a user with the Student role: the row action menu does not contain 'Edit Roles' (or the action is disabled). Attempting to access role editing is blocked; no role-editing UI opens and the participant's roles remain unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Student role cannot use 'Enrol Selected' bulk action
preconditions: User is on the Participants page
steps: 1. Select the checkbox for <participant> in the Participants table
2. Open the bulk actions menu
3. Attempt to click the 'Enrol Selected' bulk action
test_cases.expected_result: 'Enrol Selected' bulk action is not available to a user with the Student role: the bulk actions menu does not show 'Enrol Selected' (or it is disabled). The enrolment action is blocked; no enrolment occurs and selected participants' enrolment status remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Very long text in Condition Value (200+ chars)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click "+ Add condition"
2. Select <an attribute> in Attribute_Select
3. Enter <a very long string (200+ characters)> in Value
4. Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Apply filters succeeds; participants list updates to reflect the filter; the Conditions row's Value cell displays the full entered string (no truncation) and the UI shows the exact text as entered

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and emoji in Condition Value
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click "+ Add condition"
2. Select <an attribute> in Attribute_Select
3. Enter <a string containing special characters and emoji/unicode> in Value
4. Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Apply filters succeeds; participants list updates to reflect the filter; the Conditions row's Value cell displays the exact special characters and emoji (no encoding artifacts), and no UI error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Condition Value is trimmed on save/display
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click "+ Add condition"
2. Select <an attribute> in Attribute_Select
3. Enter <a value with leading and trailing whitespace> in Value
4. Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Apply filters succeeds; participants list updates to reflect the filter; the Conditions row's Value cell displays the value with leading/trailing whitespace trimmed (visible saved value has no extra spaces)

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle Any checkbox then Apply filters
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click the Any_Toggle checkbox to check it
2. Click the Any_Toggle checkbox to uncheck it
3. Click the Any_Toggle checkbox to check it again
4. Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Apply filters succeeds; the participants list is filtered using the final state of the Any toggle (checked); no duplicate condition rows are created and the filter UI remains stable with the Any toggle reflecting its final state

test_cases.test_case: Expand a course to reveal its activity grade rows
preconditions: Grades page is open with at least one course <Course with activities> collapsed
steps: 1. On the Grades page, locate the group header for <Course with activities>
2. Click the 'Expand/Collapse Course' control on the course group header to expand the course
test_cases.expected_result: Activity rows for <Course with activities> become visible, indented beneath the course header, showing columns: Grade item, Calculated weight, Grade, Range, Percentage, Feedback, and Contribution to course total

test_cases.test_case: Collapse an expanded course to hide its activity rows
preconditions: Grades page is open with <Course with activities> currently expanded
steps: 1. On the Grades page, locate the group header for <Course with activities>
2. Click the 'Expand/Collapse Course' control on the course group header to collapse the course
test_cases.expected_result: Activity rows for <Course with activities> are hidden and only the course header row remains visible

test_cases.test_case: See the AGGREGATION Course total row showing the cumulative grade across weighted items
preconditions: Grades page is open with at least one course <Course with activities> (expanded if necessary to show activities)
steps: 1. If the course is collapsed, click the 'Expand/Collapse Course' control to ensure activities are visible
2. Scroll to the bottom of the course grade table
test_cases.expected_result: An 'AGGREGATION Course total' row is visible showing the cumulative values for Calculated weight total, Grade total or summary, Percentage total and Contribution total that represent the cumulative grade across all weighted items

test_cases.test_case: Display '–' for an activity that has not yet been graded
preconditions: Grades page is open with <Course with activities> expanded and containing at least one activity <Activity with no grade yet>
steps: 1. On the Grades page, locate <Activity with no grade yet> within the activities list for the course
2. Observe the Grade column value for <Activity with no grade yet>
test_cases.expected_result: The Grade column for <Activity with no grade yet> displays '–' to indicate it is not yet graded

test_cases.test_case: Grades page shows only the logged-in student's grades and not other students' grades
preconditions: Grades page is open for a course that contains grades for multiple students in the system (other students' grades exist in the backend)
steps: 1. On the Grades page, view the grade table for the course
test_cases.expected_result: The grade table displays only rows corresponding to the logged-in student's grade items; rows or entries belonging to other students are not present in the table

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Grades page
steps: 1. Ensure the user session is logged out
2. Navigate to the Grades page URL (attempt to open the Grades User Report)
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the app redirects to the Login page; the Grades User Report is not displayed; the Login form is visible (user is not able to view any grade rows).

test_cases.test_case: User with non-Student role cannot view Grades report
preconditions: User is authenticated with a role other than Student (e.g., Teacher or Admin)
steps: 1. Log in as a user with a non-Student role
2. Attempt to navigate to the Grades page via the application navigation or by entering the Grades page URL directly
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the Grades User Report is not rendered for this user; the Grades navigation item is not present for the role or, if direct URL was used, the page displays an authorization error and does not show any grade rows.

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot view another student's grades
steps: 1. Log in as <Student A>
2. Attempt to access the Grades view for <Student B> (for example by entering a URL or selecting a link intended for another student)
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the page does not display <Student B>'s grades; no grade rows belonging to <Student B> are shown. The user either remains on their own Grades view or sees an authorization/error message and cannot view other students' grade data.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid expand/collapse toggle stability for a course group
preconditions: Test data contains at least one Course group with >0 Activities
steps: 1. Log in as Student
2. Navigate to the Grades page
3. Locate a Course group row (Course header) in the table
4. Click the Course's Expand/Collapse Course toggle rapidly (more than 5 clicks) finishing with a click to expand
5. Observe the Activities rows displayed under the Course
6. Click the Course's Expand/Collapse Course toggle rapidly (more than 5 clicks) finishing with a click to collapse
7. Observe the Activities rows are hidden
test_cases.expected_result: Rapid toggling succeeds: after step 4 the Activities rows are visible exactly once (no duplicate or ghost rows), the table layout remains intact and no JavaScript error dialog appears; after step 6 the Activities rows are hidden and the Course header remains interactive (UI remains responsive) — test succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Long Feedback text display (very long >200 characters) in an Activity row
preconditions: Test data contains at least one Course with an Activity whose Feedback text length exceeds 200 characters
steps: 1. Log in as Student
2. Navigate to the Grades page
3. Locate the Course row that contains the Activity with the long Feedback
4. Click the Course's Expand/Collapse Course toggle to reveal Activities
5. Observe the Feedback cell for the Activity
6. If a 'Show more' / expansion control is present in the Feedback cell, click that control
7. Observe the Feedback cell after expansion
test_cases.expected_result: The UI succeeds in handling long Feedback: initially the Feedback cell shows a truncated preview with a visible 'Show more' or ellipsis indicator; clicking 'Show more' expands to show the full Feedback text without layout overflow or loss of other table rows; collapsing restores the truncated preview — test succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Rendering and interaction with Course and Grade item names containing special characters and emoji
preconditions: Test data contains a Course_Name and at least one Grade item whose text includes special Unicode characters and emoji
steps: 1. Log in as Student
2. Navigate to the Grades page
3. Locate the Course header whose Course_Name contains special characters/emoji
4. Visually inspect the Course header text rendering
5. Click the Course's Expand/Collapse Course toggle
6. Inspect the Grade item rows under that Course for correct rendering of special characters/emoji
test_cases.expected_result: Rendering of special Unicode characters and emoji succeeds: Course header and Grade item text display the special characters/emoji uncorrupted (no replacement glyphs or truncated code sequences), and Expand/Collapse interaction succeeds with those rows displayed correctly — test succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Aggregation row rounding/precision when individual Percentage values have high decimal precision
preconditions: Test data contains a Course with multiple Activities whose Percentage values include many decimal places (more precision than typical displayed precision)
steps: 1. Log in as Student
2. Navigate to the Grades page
3. Locate the Course with Activities that have high-precision Percentage values
4. Click the Course's Expand/Collapse Course toggle to reveal all Activity Percentage values
5. Manually sum the visible Percentage column values for the activities in that Course (observe the values as shown in the UI)
6. Observe the AGGREGATION Course total Percentage_total cell at the bottom of that Course group
test_cases.expected_result: Display of aggregated Percentage succeeds: the AGGREGATION Course total cell shows the cumulative Percentage consistent with the UI's displayed per-activity values after the UI's rounding policy (e.g., the aggregation equals the sum of the displayed per-activity percentages when both sides are rounded to the same visible precision); the aggregation cell is visible and shows no truncation or overflow — test succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Add submission using Online Text editor (assignment allows online text)
preconditions: Submission status == No submissions have been made yet
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click Add submission in the Submission status section
3. Enter <valid text> in the Online Text Editor
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row displays 'Submitted for grading' in the Submission status section and the submitted online text is visible in the submission summary

test_cases.test_case: Add submission using File Upload area (assignment allows file upload)
preconditions: Submission status == No submissions have been made yet
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click Add submission in the Submission status section
3. Click Upload / Select file and choose a <valid file> in the OS file picker
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row displays 'Submitted for grading' in the Submission status section and the Submitted files list shows the <uploaded file> in the submission summary

test_cases.test_case: Open submission form and Cancel without saving
preconditions: Assignment allows_online_text == true or Assignment allows_file_upload == true
Submission status == No submissions have been made yet
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click Add submission in the Submission status section
3. (Optional) Enter <valid text> in the Online Text Editor or upload a <valid file>
4. Click Cancel
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form is closed and the Submission status row continues to show 'No submissions have been made yet'

test_cases.test_case: View an existing submitted submission (Submitted for grading state) in read-only mode
preconditions: Student has an existing submission (online text and/or files)
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click View submission in the Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: A read-only view opens showing the student's submitted content (online text and/or submitted files); edit controls and input fields are not present in this view

test_cases.test_case: Edit an existing submission before due date when resubmission is permitted
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click Edit submission in the Submission status section
3. Update the online text by entering <updated text> and/or replace the file by uploading a <replacement file>
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form closes and the Submission status row still shows 'Submitted for grading'; the Submission summary displays the updated <online text> and/or the updated <uploaded file>

test_cases.test_case: View a graded submission showing earned grade and written feedback
preconditions: A grade and written feedback exist for the student's submission
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click View submission in the Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: Read-only view opens showing the earned grade and the written feedback within the submission view; Submission status row displays 'Graded'

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Edit submission when due date has passed
preconditions: Student has an existing submission in state 'Submitted for grading'
Assignment due date is in the past (due date not passed precondition is false)
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: Edit submission action is blocked: the 'Edit submission' button is not available or is disabled and clicking it does nothing; the existing submission remains in state 'Submitted for grading' (no edit form opens and submission is not updated).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Edit submission when teacher forbids resubmission
preconditions: Student has an existing submission in state 'Submitted for grading'
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: Edit submission action is blocked: the 'Edit submission' button is not available or is disabled and clicking it does nothing; the existing submission remains unchanged (no edit form opens and submission is not updated).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Edit submission when grading status is Graded
preconditions: Student has an existing submission whose Grading status == Graded
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above precondition
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: Edit submission action is blocked: the 'Edit submission' button is not available or is disabled and clicking it does nothing; the submission remains in Graded state and is not updated (no edit form opens).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Add submission when a submission already exists (state = Submitted for grading)
preconditions: Student has an existing submission in state 'Submitted for grading'
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page showing Submission status 'Submitted for grading'
2. Attempt to locate and click the 'Add submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: Add submission action is not available in this state: the 'Add submission' button is not visible (or is disabled) and the user cannot open the submission form; no new submission is created and the existing submission remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt View submission when no submissions have been made yet
preconditions: Assignment shows Submission status 'No submissions have been made yet'
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page showing 'No submissions have been made yet'
2. Attempt to locate and click a 'View submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: View submission action is not available in this state: there is no 'View submission' link or it is disabled; no submission view opens because no submission exists.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Edit submission (open form) when one of multiple preconditions fails (combined preconditions enforcement)
preconditions: Student has an existing submission in state 'Submitted for grading'
At least one of the Edit submission preconditions is false (e.g., due date passed OR teacher forbids resubmission OR grading status != 'Not graded')
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page with the above preconditions
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: Edit submission action is blocked when any required precondition is not met: the 'Edit submission' button is not available or is disabled and clicking it does nothing; the submission remains unchanged (no edit form opens and no update occurs).

test_cases.test_case: Edit allowed when due date equals today (same-day boundary)
preconditions: An existing submission exists with Submission status == Submitted for grading
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action in the Submission State Action Bar
3. Verify the Submission_Form opens
4. In the Online_Text_Editor, enter a small edit to the existing text
5. Click the 'Submit' button on the Submission_Form
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Edit submission' opens the Submission_Form and submitting the form succeeds; the existing submission is updated (Submission status remains 'Submitted for grading') and the 'Last modified' timestamp on the Assignment page updates to reflect the edit.

test_cases.test_case: Edit blocked when due date has just passed (one-day past boundary)
preconditions: An existing submission exists with Submission status == Submitted for grading
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Attempt to click the 'Edit submission' action in the Submission State Action Bar (if visible)
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Edit submission' action is not available or clicking it is blocked; an inline message or disabled control indicates the action is unavailable because the due date has passed — the attempt to open the Submission_Form is blocked / error shown.

test_cases.test_case: Edit blocked when teacher disallows resubmission (permission boundary)
preconditions: An existing submission exists with Submission status == Submitted for grading
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Attempt to click the 'Edit submission' action in the Submission State Action Bar (if visible)
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Edit submission' action is not available or clicking it is blocked; an inline message or disabled control indicates resubmissions are not permitted — the attempt to open the Submission_Form is blocked / error shown.

test_cases.test_case: Immediate edit after Add submission updates the same submission (no duplicate) when done rapidly
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for the assignment with the above preconditions
2. Click 'Add submission' in the Submission State Action Bar
3. In the Submission_Form, enter text in the Online_Text_Editor
4. Click the 'Submit' button
5. After the success transition completes and the page shows the new submission state, immediately click 'Edit submission'
6. In the Submission_Form, change the online text
7. Click the 'Submit' button again
test_cases.expected_result: The first submit succeeds and creates the submission; the immediate second submit (edit) also succeeds and updates the existing submission (no duplicate submission is created). The Submission status remains 'Submitted for grading', the Submission summary shows a single submission row, and 'Last modified' updates to the time of the edit.

test_cases.test_case: Very long online text (>200 characters) entered in Online_Text_Editor is saved and displayed
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page and click 'Add submission'
2. In the Online_Text_Editor, paste a very long text string (200+ characters)
3. Click the 'Submit' button on the Submission_Form
4. Open the read-only submission view (or view the submission detail on the Assignment page)
test_cases.expected_result: The form submits successfully; the submission detail view displays the full entered text (no truncation) — the long content is visible in the submission detail.

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Online_Text_Editor is trimmed when saved
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page and click 'Add submission'
2. In the Online_Text_Editor, enter text with leading and trailing spaces (e.g., spaces before and after the substantive text)
3. Click the 'Submit' button on the Submission_Form
4. Open the read-only submission view (or view the submission detail on the Assignment page)
test_cases.expected_result: The form submits successfully; the saved submission text shown in the detail view has leading and trailing whitespace removed (leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed).

test_cases.test_case: File upload with Unicode/emoji characters in filename is accepted and filename preserved
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page and click 'Add submission'
2. In the File_Upload_Area, attach a file whose filename includes Unicode characters or emoji
3. Click the 'Submit' button on the Submission_Form
4. Open the submission detail view on the Assignment page
test_cases.expected_result: The submit action succeeds; the submission detail shows the uploaded file with the filename preserved (Unicode/emoji characters are displayed as uploaded).

test_cases.test_case: Assignments section is expanded by default and shows the assignments table
preconditions: <At least one assignment exists in the course>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments section is expanded by default; the Assignments table is visible with columns Name, Due date, and Submission status, and at least one row is listed with a clickable Name link

test_cases.test_case: Clicking an assignment Name opens that activity's page
preconditions: <At least one assignment exists in the course with a visible Name link>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Click the Name link for <assignment> in the Assignments table
test_cases.expected_result: The activity detail page for <assignment> is displayed (the browser navigates to the activity page); the activity page header shows <assignment>

test_cases.test_case: Collapsed activity sections (Forums and Resources) expand when their arrows are clicked
preconditions: <Forums and Resources sections exist in the course Activities accordion>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Click the expand arrow on the Forums section
3. Click the expand arrow on the Resources section
test_cases.expected_result: Forums section expands and its content area becomes visible; Resources section expands and its content area becomes visible

test_cases.test_case: An additional activity type appears as a collapsed, expandable section
preconditions: <An additional activity type <activity type> exists in the course>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Locate the additional <activity type> section in the accordion
3. Click the expand arrow on the <activity type> section
test_cases.expected_result: An additional <activity type> section appears collapsed in the Activities accordion; after clicking its expand arrow the <activity type> content area becomes visible

test_cases.test_case: Activity Name link does not navigate to activity page
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
Assignments section contains at least one activity row with a Name link
steps: 1. Locate the Assignments section on the Activities page
2. In the Assignments table, click the Name link for <an activity row>
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Name' link does not navigate to the activity's page; the Activities page remains displayed and the Activity detail page is not opened; the browser URL remains unchanged (no navigation occurs). This blocks access to the activity page via the Name link.

test_cases.test_case: Forums section arrow does not expand the collapsed section
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
Forums section is present and collapsed by default
steps: 1. Locate the Forums section on the Activities page
2. Click the collapse/expand arrow for the Forums section
test_cases.expected_result: The Forums section remains collapsed and its content is not revealed; the collapse/expand arrow does not change state (no expansion occurs) and no activities or content are displayed under Forums. The section should have expanded but did not, blocking access to Forum items via the accordion.

test_cases.test_case: Very long activity Name in table link
preconditions: An activity exists whose Name is a string longer than typical table width (enter <very long string> as the activity Name) and it is listed under the Assignments section
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Locate the Assignments section (default expanded)
3. Observe the Name cell for the activity with the very long Name
4. Click the activity Name link
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: The Assignments table renders the Name cell without breaking layout (table cell displays truncated text with ellipsis or wrapped text) and clicking the Name navigates to the activity detail page; the activity detail page shows the full activity Name exactly as created (no characters lost).

test_cases.test_case: Activity Name containing special characters and emoji
preconditions: An activity exists whose Name contains special characters and emoji (enter <string with emoji and special characters>) and it is listed under the Assignments section
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Locate the Assignments section
3. Observe the Name cell for the activity with special characters/emoji
4. Click the activity Name link
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: The Name cell displays the special characters and emoji without replacement characters; clicking the Name navigates to the activity detail page which displays the exact same Name string including special characters and emoji.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid expand/collapse of a collapsed section (Forums) does not corrupt UI state
preconditions: Activities page has the Forums section present (no requirement for entries in it); Forums is collapsed by default per spec
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Verify Forums section is collapsed by default
3. Click the Forums section expand arrow
4. Immediately click the Forums section collapse arrow
5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 rapidly several times, finishing with a single click to expand the Forums section
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: UI remains stable with no visual corruption; final visible state matches the last user action (Forums is expanded after the final expand click); section content becomes visible and the page remains responsive.

test_cases.test_case: Display of activities with Due_Date = <today> and Due_Date = <far future>
preconditions: Two activities exist: one with Due_Date = <today>, the other with Due_Date = <far future date> (enter these dates when creating activities). Both are listed under Assignments.
steps: 1. Navigate to the Activities page
2. Locate the Assignments table
3. Find the row for the activity with Due_Date = <today> and observe the Due_Date cell
4. Find the row for the activity with Due_Date = <far future date> and observe the Due_Date cell
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Both rows appear in the Assignments table; the Due_Date cell for the activity with Due_Date = <today> displays today's date (or the UI's 'today' indicator) and the Due_Date cell for the activity with Due_Date = <far future date> displays the calendar date correctly; no error or omission is shown for either date.

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Profile form from Profile page
preconditions: Profile page is open showing <own profile>
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link in the User details card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Edit Profile form

test_cases.test_case: Update first and last name and save
preconditions: Profile page is open showing <own profile>
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link in the User details card to open the Edit Profile form
2. In the General panel, enter <new first name> in the First name field
3. Enter <new last name> in the Last name field
4. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Profile form closes and Profile page displays the updated full name '<new first name> <new last name>'

test_cases.test_case: Upload a new user picture and save
preconditions: Profile page is open showing <own profile>
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link in the User details card to open the Edit Profile form
2. Expand the 'User picture' panel if collapsed
3. Click the upload control for New picture upload
4. Select a <valid image file> in the OS file dialog
5. (Optional) Enter <picture description> in the Picture description field
6. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Profile form closes and Profile page displays the newly uploaded image as the user's profile picture

test_cases.test_case: Cancel edits and return to Profile page unchanged
preconditions: Profile page is open showing <own profile> with an existing Description
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link in the User details card to open the Edit Profile form
2. In the General panel, enter <proposed description> in the Description field
3. Click the 'Cancel' button
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Profile form closes and Profile page continues to show the original Description (no changes applied)

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Data retention summary from Privacy and policies card
steps: 1. Click the 'Data retention summary' link in the Privacy and policies card
test_cases.expected_result: The browser navigates to the Data retention summary page (redirect occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Open an enrolled course profile from Course details
preconditions: Profile page is open showing the Enrolled courses list
steps: 1. Click an <enrolled course> link under the Enrolled courses repeating list in the Course details card
test_cases.expected_result: The browser navigates to the selected course profile page (redirect occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Open Browser sessions from Reports card
steps: 1. Click the 'Browser sessions' link in the Reports card
test_cases.expected_result: The browser navigates to the Browser sessions page (redirect occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Leave required text field blank (representative: First name)
steps: 1. In the Edit Profile form, clear the <First name> field (leave it blank)
2. Fill all other required fields with valid values
3. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: First name field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit and the profile is not saved

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
steps: 1. Clear or leave empty all required fields in the Edit Profile form (e.g., <First name>, <Email address>, <Timezone>)
2. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on each required field (examples: <First name>, <Email address>, <Timezone>) indicating they are required; the form does not submit and the profile is not saved

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid email format in Email_address field
steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> in the Email_address field
2. Ensure all other required fields contain valid values
3. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Email_address field displays an inline validation error indicating the email format is invalid; the form does not submit and the profile is not saved

test_cases.test_case: Edit profile link not visible when viewing another student's profile (precondition violation)
preconditions: Authenticated as a student who is NOT the profile owner
Viewing the Profile page for <another student>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page for <another student>
2. Inspect the User details card for the 'Edit profile' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Edit profile' link is not visible on the User details card for profiles other than the authenticated user; the Edit Profile form does not open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Update profile' action when not the profile owner (precondition on submit_actions)
preconditions: Authenticated as a student who is NOT the profile owner
steps: 1. Navigate directly to the Edit Profile form URL for <another student's profile>
2. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: a visible access-denied message is shown on the page indicating the user does not have permission to modify this profile; no profile changes are saved and the user remains off the affected profile edit state

test_cases.test_case: Very long text in Description (>200 characters)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the "Edit profile" link on the Profile page
2. Expand the "General" collapsible panel if collapsed
3. In the Description field enter a very long string (>200 characters)
4. Click the "Update profile" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; profile changes are saved and the Profile page displays the Description exactly as entered (the long string) — success is visible on the Profile page (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Names containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the "Edit profile" link on the Profile page
2. Expand the "General" collapsible panel if collapsed
3. In the First name field enter a string containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters
4. In the Last name field enter a string containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters
5. Click the "Update profile" button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; profile changes are saved and the Profile page displays the full name including the emoji and non-Latin characters exactly as entered (the circular initials/icon and full name reflect those characters) — succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in City/town (trim behavior)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the "Edit profile" link on the Profile page
2. Expand the "General" collapsible panel if collapsed
3. In the City/town field enter a value with leading and trailing spaces
4. Click the "Update profile" button
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace is trimmed on save; profile changes succeed and the Profile page displays the City/town without the leading or trailing spaces (saved value shown has no extra spaces) — succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of "Update profile" causes duplicate submissions
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the "Edit profile" link on the Profile page
2. Expand the "General" collapsible panel if collapsed
3. Change the Last name field to a new value
4. Click the "Update profile" button twice rapidly (two clicks within a short interval)
test_cases.expected_result: First submission succeeds and profile changes are saved; the UI prevents or ignores the immediate second click so no duplicate save or duplicate entity is created. A single success indicator/message is visible and the Profile page displays the updated Last name once (first submission succeeds; second attempt is ignored/blocked).

test_cases.test_case: Clicking 'Log out' redirects to Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Login page is displayed (the application redirects to the Login page)

test_cases.test_case: Logout button is not available to unauthenticated users
steps: 1. Open the application as an unauthenticated user
2. Inspect the page header/controls for a 'Log out' button
3. If a 'Log out' control is visible, attempt to click it
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Log out' button is not visible on the page for unauthenticated users. If the unauthenticated user attempts to invoke the Logout action directly, the system blocks the action by redirecting to the Login page; no session termination occurs because there was no authenticated session.

test_cases.test_case: Access to protected page is blocked after logout (requires re-authentication)
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. After redirect completes, attempt to navigate to a protected page that requires authentication
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the protected page is blocked: the system redirects to the Login page and the protected content is not displayed. The protected page shows the login prompt and requires re-authentication; the previously authenticated session remains terminated.

test_cases.test_case: Second logout attempt after session already terminated is blocked / no-op
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Click the 'Log out' button to terminate the session
3. After being redirected to the Login page (session unauthenticated), attempt to trigger the Logout action again (e.g., click any residual 'Log out' control or invoke the logout action)
test_cases.expected_result: The second Logout attempt is blocked or treated as a no-op: the UI remains on or redirects to the Login page, no error exposing internal state is shown, the 'Log out' button is not available for an unauthenticated session, and no additional session-state change occurs (session remains unauthenticated).

test_cases.test_case: Double-click the Log out button rapidly
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
steps: 1. Locate the 'Log out' button
2. Click the 'Log out' button twice in rapid succession (two clicks within 1 second)
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single logout action is applied: the session termination and redirect to the Login page occur once; no duplicate errors or duplicate redirects are shown; user is unauthenticated and access to protected pages requires re-authentication — logout succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Click Log out then immediately press browser Back
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
2. After the redirect to the Login page completes, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation back to the protected page is blocked: the Login page is displayed (or the browser stays/redirects to Login) and protected content is not visible; access to protected pages remains blocked until re-authentication — access is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Open a protected page in a second tab, log out in the first tab, then refresh the second tab
preconditions: Protected page is open in Tab A and Tab B (same authenticated session)
steps: 1. In Tab A, click the 'Log out' button
2. In Tab B, click the browser Refresh/Reload button on the protected page
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the protected page in Tab B after logout is blocked: the page redirects to the Login page (or displays a login prompt) and protected content is not accessible until the user re-authenticates — access is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Initiate a protected-page action that triggers a background request, then click Log out while the request is in flight
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page with an action that sends a background request (e.g., save, submit) to the server
steps: 1. Trigger the background action (start the request)
2. While the request is still in flight, click the 'Log out' button
3. Wait for the background request to complete (server response arrives)
test_cases.expected_result: The logout action terminates the session and redirects to the Login page immediately; any late-arriving success response from the in-flight request does not render protected content or re-authenticate the user in the UI; the user remains unauthenticated and protected UI is not shown — logout succeeds
